problem: Ten 6-sided dice are rolled. What is the probability that exactly three of the dice show a 1? Express your answer as a decimal rounded to the nearest thousandth.

classified_questions.question: How would you define probability in the context of this problem?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: What is the total number of possible outcomes when ten 6-sided dice are rolled?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How many ways are there to choose exactly three dice to show a 1 out of ten dice?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can you explain why each selected dice showing a 1 has a probability of $\left(\dfrac{1}{6}\right)^3$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Why is the probability of the remaining seven dice not showing a 1 $\left(\dfrac{5}{6}\right)^7$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Are all the outcomes of the three selected dice and the remaining seven dice mutually exclusive?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: How do we calculate the total probability of exactly three dice showing a 1 using the previously discussed probabilities and combinations?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can you simplify the expression $\dfrac{120\cdot5^7\cdot1^3}{6^{10}}$ and round it to the nearest thousandth?
classified_questions.label: b
